candidates.why: A general D&A line is present, but there is no selected COGS reference bucket and no wording tying this D&A specifically to cost of sales / cost of revenues. Adding it to COGS would be unsupported and risks misclassification.
Component of total D&A, but not labelled as COGS-related. Also inferior to the summary D&A row if any D&A were to be added.
Component of total D&A, but not labelled as COGS-related and should not be used over the summary row.
Balancing-value line is not an operating D&A bucket to add to COGS.
Candidate label exists but no visible amounts and no evidence it is a separate COGS-related D&A line.

variables.notes: No COGS row was selected in the earlier mapping, and the income statement excerpt provides no D&A row explicitly associated with cost of sales / cost of revenues. Conservative no-add to avoid misclassification or double counting.

candidates.why: Best summary D&A row. It appears as a separate operating line alongside the selected XSGA reference rows ('Operatingexpenses' and 'Salaries and related expenses'), rather than as an indented sub-component of them. Therefore it looks outside the selected XSGA bucket and should be added separately.
A component of total D&A for some years only. Do not use when the summary row 'Depreciation and amortization' is available, to avoid undercoverage or double counting.
A component of total D&A for some years only. Inferior to the summary D&A row and should not be selected separately when the total exists.
Balancing-value line is not the operating D&A expense to add; likely a residual/check line.
No visible amounts and not needed because the summary D&A row exists.

variables.notes: Selected XSGA bucket from the prior step is 'Operatingexpenses' plus 'Salaries and related expenses'. The separate line 'Depreciation and amortization' appears at the same operating-expense level and before the broader total 'Operating Expense', so it appears outside the selected XSGA rows rather than embedded within them. Summary row chosen over component rows to avoid double counting.
